mechanism_narrative: A persistently low FEDFUNDS level relative to CPIAUCSL inflation implies a deeply negative real policy rate, which continues to support risk asset valuations by keeping the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows suppressed, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC between real rate proxy (FEDFUNDS minus CPI YoY) and forward 20d returns is below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or negative real rates coincide with negative forward returns in more than 55% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points relative to its 3-month trailing minimum, this Sahm-rule-like threshold signals the onset of labor market deterioration that historically precedes recessions, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following quarter as earnings expectations are revised down.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.3pp above its 3-month trailing minimum
failure_condition: Forward 60-day SPX returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances, or the signal fails to precede any labor-market-driven drawdown within 12 months of activation across the sample.

mechanism_narrative: A joint decline in HOUST and RSAFS reflects simultaneous weakening in interest-rate-sensitive housing activity and broad consumer spending, a combination indicating a genuine demand contraction rather than sector-specific noise, and predicts negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint declines in HOUST and RSAFS are followed by positive 20d SPX returns in more than 55% of instances, or the interaction signal shows no incremental predictive power over either series individually (interaction coefficient not significant at p<0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Positive month-over-month momentum in INDPRO signals expanding industrial output and corporate production capacity, which mechanically supports near-term corporate revenue growth expectations and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC between INDPRO momentum and forward 5-day SPX returns falls below 0.02 over any rolling 1-year window, or positive INDPRO momentum periods do not outperform negative momentum periods at p>0.10.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle defined by consecutive FEDFUNDS increases, continued upside surprises in CPIAUCSL force markets to price additional rate hikes, compressing equity valuation multiples and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter; this relationship reverses once tightening pauses.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS has risen in each of the last 3 meetings AND CPIAUCSL YoY change exceeds 4%
failure_condition: During periods meeting both trigger conditions, forward 60-day SPX returns are positive in more than 50% of instances, or the effect disappears once controlling for prior 60-day realized returns.

mechanism_narrative: A deceleration in PAYEMS month-over-month growth below its trailing 6-month average signals cooling labor demand, which reduces expected consumer income growth and corporate hiring-driven revenue expansion, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 1-month change falls below its trailing 6-month average change
failure_condition: SPX forward 20-day returns are positive in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the signal shows no significant difference in mean forward return versus non-triggered periods (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When GDPC1 quarterly growth decelerates while RSAFS simultaneously contracts on a 3-month basis, the combination confirms a broad-based demand slowdown affecting both aggregate output and retail consumption, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter as earnings guidance is cut.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 QoQ annualized growth < 1% AND RSAFS 3-month change < 0
failure_condition: Joint slowdown conditions are followed by positive 60-day forward SPX returns in more than 50% of instances, or GDPC1 revisions materially alter the trigger classification in more than 30% of historical cases.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-rate regime where FEDFUNDS exceeds 4%, a decline in INDPRO reflects tightening credit conditions constraining industrial output, and this supply-side weakness is quickly priced by equity markets, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days; outside high-rate regimes the same INDPRO decline has muted effect.
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with restrictive policy rate
failure_condition: Under the high-rate trigger condition, forward 5-day SPX returns are positive in more than 55% of instances, or the effect size is not significantly different from INDPRO declines occurring when FEDFUNDS <= 4%.
